title: Variance
description: Practice calculating both sample and population variances.
topic_path: Variance and standard deviation of a sample

assessment_items.question_content:
You have found the following ages (in years) of all $4$ zebras at your local zoo:

 $\qquad14,\enspace 15,\enspace 9,\enspace 1$

 ** What is the average age of the zebras at your zoo? What is the variance?**  
*Round your answers to the nearest tenth.*

Average age:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 1]] years old 

  Variance:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 2]] years$^2$
hints: Because we have data for all $4$ zebras at the zoo, we are able to calculate the population mean $(\blue{\mu})$ and population variance $(\red{\sigma^2})$.

To find the population mean, add up the values of all $4$ ages and divide by $4$. 

  $ \blue{\mu} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{N}} x_i}{\green{N}} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{4}} x_i}{\green{4}} $
$ \blue{\mu} \quad = \quad \dfrac{14 + 15 + 9 + 1}{\green{4}} \quad = \quad \blue{9.75\text{ years old}} $

Find the squared deviations from the mean for each zebra. 

 Age
 
 $x_i$ | Distance from the mean $(x_i - \blue{\mu})$ | $(x_i - \blue{\mu})^2$ 
-|-|-
 $14$ years | $4.25$ years | $18.06$ years$^2$ 
 $15$ years | $5.25$ years | $27.56$ years$^2$ 
 $9$ years | $-0.75$ years | $0.56$ years$^2$ 
 $1$ year | $-8.75$ years | $76.56$ years$^2$

Because we used the population mean $(\blue{\mu})$ to compute the squared deviations from the mean, we can find the population variance $(\color{red}{\sigma^2})$, without introducing any bias, by simply averaging the squared deviations from the mean: 

  $ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{N}} (x_i - \blue{\mu})^2}{\green{N}} $
$ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{18.06} + \color{purple}{27.56} + \color{purple}{0.56} + \color{purple}{76.56}} {\green{4}} $
$ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{122.74}}{\green{4}} \quad = \quad \color{red}{30.69\text{ years}^2} $
The average zebra at the zoo is $9.8$ years old.   The population variance is $30.7$ years$^2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
You have found the following ages (in years) of all $4$ zebras at your local zoo:

 $\qquad7,\enspace 1,\enspace 9,\enspace 14$

 ** What is the average age of the zebras at your zoo? What is the variance?**  
*Round your answers to the nearest tenth.*

Average age:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 1]] years old 

  Variance:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 2]] years$^2$
hints: Because we have data for all $4$ zebras at the zoo, we are able to calculate the population mean $(\blue{\mu})$ and population variance $(\red{\sigma^2})$.

To find the population mean, add up the values of all $4$ ages and divide by $4$. 

  $ \blue{\mu} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{N}} x_i}{\green{N}} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{4}} x_i}{\green{4}} $
$ \blue{\mu} \quad = \quad \dfrac{7 + 1 + 9 + 14}{\green{4}} \quad = \quad \blue{7.75\text{ years old}} $

Find the squared deviations from the mean for each zebra. 

 Age
 
 $x_i$ | Distance from the mean $(x_i - \blue{\mu})$ | $(x_i - \blue{\mu})^2$ 
-|-|-
 $7$ years | $-0.75$ years | $0.56$ years$^2$ 
 $1$ year | $-6.75$ years | $45.56$ years$^2$ 
 $9$ years | $1.25$ years | $1.56$ years$^2$ 
 $14$ years | $6.25$ years | $39.06$ years$^2$

Because we used the population mean $(\blue{\mu})$ to compute the squared deviations from the mean, we can find the population variance $(\color{red}{\sigma^2})$, without introducing any bias, by simply averaging the squared deviations from the mean: 

  $ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{N}} (x_i - \blue{\mu})^2}{\green{N}} $
$ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{0.56} + \color{purple}{45.56} + \color{purple}{1.56} + \color{purple}{39.06}} {\green{4}} $
$ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{86.74}}{\green{4}} \quad = \quad \color{red}{21.69\text{ years}^2} $
The average zebra at the zoo is $7.8$ years old.   The population variance is $21.7$ years$^2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
You have found the following ages (in years) of all $6$ lions at your local zoo:

 $\qquad13,\enspace 2,\enspace 1,\enspace 5,\enspace 2,\enspace 7$

 ** What is the average age of the lions at your zoo? What is the variance?**  
*Round your answers to the nearest tenth.*

Average age:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 1]] years old 

  Variance:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 2]] years$^2$
hints: Because we have data for all $6$ lions at the zoo, we are able to calculate the population mean $(\blue{\mu})$ and population variance $(\red{\sigma^2})$.

To find the population mean, add up the values of all $6$ ages and divide by $6$. 

  $ \blue{\mu} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{N}} x_i}{\green{N}} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{6}} x_i}{\green{6}} $
$ \blue{\mu} \quad = \quad \dfrac{13 + 2 + 1 + 5 + 2 + 7}{\green{6}} \quad = \quad \blue{5\text{ years old}} $

Find the squared deviations from the mean for each lion. 

 Age
 
 $x_i$ | Distance from the mean $(x_i - \blue{\mu})$ | $(x_i - \blue{\mu})^2$ 
-|-|-
 $13$ years | $8$ years | $64$ years$^2$ 
 $2$ years | $-3$ years | $9$ years$^2$ 
 $1$ year | $-4$ years | $16$ years$^2$ 
 $5$ years | $0$ years | $0$ years$^2$ 
 $2$ years | $-3$ years | $9$ years$^2$ 
 $7$ years | $2$ years | $4$ years$^2$

Because we used the population mean $(\blue{\mu})$ to compute the squared deviations from the mean, we can find the population variance $(\color{red}{\sigma^2})$, without introducing any bias, by simply averaging the squared deviations from the mean: 

  $ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{N}} (x_i - \blue{\mu})^2}{\green{N}} $
$ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{64} + \color{purple}{9} + \color{purple}{16} + \color{purple}{0} + \color{purple}{9} + \color{purple}{4}} {\green{6}} $
$ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{102}}{\green{6}} \quad = \quad \color{red}{17\text{ years}^2} $
The average lion at the zoo is $5$ years old.   The population variance is $17$ years$^2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
You have found the following ages (in years) of all $5$ gorillas at your local zoo:

 $\qquad8,\enspace 4,\enspace 14,\enspace 16,\enspace 8$

 ** What is the average age of the gorillas at your zoo? What is the variance?**  
*Round your answers to the nearest tenth.*

Average age:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 1]] years old 

  Variance:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 2]] years$^2$
hints: Because we have data for all $5$ gorillas at the zoo, we are able to calculate the population mean $(\blue{\mu})$ and population variance $(\red{\sigma^2})$.

To find the population mean, add up the values of all $5$ ages and divide by $5$. 

  $ \blue{\mu} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{N}} x_i}{\green{N}} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{5}} x_i}{\green{5}} $
$ \blue{\mu} \quad = \quad \dfrac{8 + 4 + 14 + 16 + 8}{\green{5}} \quad = \quad \blue{10\text{ years old}} $

Find the squared deviations from the mean for each gorilla. 

 Age
 
 $x_i$ | Distance from the mean $(x_i - \blue{\mu})$ | $(x_i - \blue{\mu})^2$ 
-|-|-
 $8$ years | $-2$ years | $4$ years$^2$ 
 $4$ years | $-6$ years | $36$ years$^2$ 
 $14$ years | $4$ years | $16$ years$^2$ 
 $16$ years | $6$ years | $36$ years$^2$ 
 $8$ years | $-2$ years | $4$ years$^2$

Because we used the population mean $(\blue{\mu})$ to compute the squared deviations from the mean, we can find the population variance $(\color{red}{\sigma^2})$, without introducing any bias, by simply averaging the squared deviations from the mean: 

  $ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{N}} (x_i - \blue{\mu})^2}{\green{N}} $
$ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{4} + \color{purple}{36} + \color{purple}{16} + \color{purple}{36} + \color{purple}{4}} {\green{5}} $
$ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{96}}{\green{5}} \quad = \quad \color{red}{19.2\text{ years}^2} $
The average gorilla at the zoo is $10$ years old.   The population variance is $19.2$ years$^2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
You have found the following ages (in years) of all $4$ gorillas at your local zoo:

 $\qquad7,\enspace 10,\enspace 15,\enspace 21$

 ** What is the average age of the gorillas at your zoo? What is the variance?**  
*Round your answers to the nearest tenth.*

Average age:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 1]] years old 

  Variance:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 2]] years$^2$
hints: Because we have data for all $4$ gorillas at the zoo, we are able to calculate the population mean $(\blue{\mu})$ and population variance $(\red{\sigma^2})$.

To find the population mean, add up the values of all $4$ ages and divide by $4$. 

  $ \blue{\mu} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{N}} x_i}{\green{N}} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{4}} x_i}{\green{4}} $
$ \blue{\mu} \quad = \quad \dfrac{7 + 10 + 15 + 21}{\green{4}} \quad = \quad \blue{13.25\text{ years old}} $

Find the squared deviations from the mean for each gorilla. 

 Age
 
 $x_i$ | Distance from the mean $(x_i - \blue{\mu})$ | $(x_i - \blue{\mu})^2$ 
-|-|-
 $7$ years | $-6.25$ years | $39.06$ years$^2$ 
 $10$ years | $-3.25$ years | $10.56$ years$^2$ 
 $15$ years | $1.75$ years | $3.06$ years$^2$ 
 $21$ years | $7.75$ years | $60.06$ years$^2$

Because we used the population mean $(\blue{\mu})$ to compute the squared deviations from the mean, we can find the population variance $(\color{red}{\sigma^2})$, without introducing any bias, by simply averaging the squared deviations from the mean: 

  $ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{N}} (x_i - \blue{\mu})^2}{\green{N}} $
$ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{39.06} + \color{purple}{10.56} + \color{purple}{3.06} + \color{purple}{60.06}} {\green{4}} $
$ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{112.74}}{\green{4}} \quad = \quad \color{red}{28.19\text{ years}^2} $
The average gorilla at the zoo is $13.3$ years old.   The population variance is $28.2$ years$^2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
You have found the following ages (in years) of all $4$ bears at your local zoo:

 $\qquad5,\enspace 4,\enspace 6,\enspace 39$

 ** What is the average age of the bears at your zoo? What is the variance?**  
*Round your answers to the nearest tenth.*

Average age:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 1]] years old 

  Variance:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 2]] years$^2$
hints: Because we have data for all $4$ bears at the zoo, we are able to calculate the population mean $(\blue{\mu})$ and population variance $(\red{\sigma^2})$.

To find the population mean, add up the values of all $4$ ages and divide by $4$. 

  $ \blue{\mu} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{N}} x_i}{\green{N}} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{4}} x_i}{\green{4}} $
$ \blue{\mu} \quad = \quad \dfrac{5 + 4 + 6 + 39}{\green{4}} \quad = \quad \blue{13.5\text{ years old}} $

Find the squared deviations from the mean for each bear. 

 Age
 
 $x_i$ | Distance from the mean $(x_i - \blue{\mu})$ | $(x_i - \blue{\mu})^2$ 
-|-|-
 $5$ years | $-8.5$ years | $72.25$ years$^2$ 
 $4$ years | $-9.5$ years | $90.25$ years$^2$ 
 $6$ years | $-7.5$ years | $56.25$ years$^2$ 
 $39$ years | $25.5$ years | $650.25$ years$^2$

Because we used the population mean $(\blue{\mu})$ to compute the squared deviations from the mean, we can find the population variance $(\color{red}{\sigma^2})$, without introducing any bias, by simply averaging the squared deviations from the mean: 

  $ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{N}} (x_i - \blue{\mu})^2}{\green{N}} $
$ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{72.25} + \color{purple}{90.25} + \color{purple}{56.25} + \color{purple}{650.25}} {\green{4}} $
$ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{869}}{\green{4}} \quad = \quad \color{red}{217.25\text{ years}^2} $
The average bear at the zoo is $13.5$ years old.   The population variance is $217.3$ years$^2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
You have found the following ages (in years) of all $4$ tigers at your local zoo:

 $\qquad22,\enspace 13,\enspace 18,\enspace 16$

 **What is the average age of the tigers at your zoo? What is the variance?**  
*Round your answers to the nearest tenth.*

Average age:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 1]] years old 

  Variance:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 2]] years$^2$
hints: Because we have data for all $4$ tigers at the zoo, we are able to calculate the population mean $(\blue{\mu})$ and population variance $(\red{\sigma^2})$.

To find the population mean, add up the values of all $4$ ages and divide by $4$. 

  $ \blue{\mu} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{N}} x_i}{\green{N}} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{4}} x_i}{\green{4}} $
$ \blue{\mu} \quad = \quad \dfrac{22 + 13 + 18 + 16}{\green{4}} \quad = \quad \blue{17.25\text{ years old}} $

Find the squared deviations from the mean for each tiger. 

 Age
 
 $x_i$ | Distance from the mean $(x_i - \blue{\mu})$ | $(x_i - \blue{\mu})^2$ 
-|-|-
 $22$ years | $4.75$ years | $22.56$ years$^2$ 
 $13$ years | $-4.25$ years | $18.06$ years$^2$ 
 $18$ years | $0.75$ years | $0.56$ years$^2$ 
 $16$ years | $-1.25$ years | $1.56$ years$^2$

Because we used the population mean $(\blue{\mu})$ to compute the squared deviations from the mean, we can find the population variance $(\color{red}{\sigma^2})$, without introducing any bias, by simply averaging the squared deviations from the mean: 

  $ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{N}} (x_i - \blue{\mu})^2}{\green{N}} $
$ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{22.56} + \color{purple}{18.06} + \color{purple}{0.56} + \color{purple}{1.56}} {\green{4}} $
$ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{42.74}}{\green{4}} \quad = \quad \color{red}{10.69\text{ years}^2} $
The average tiger at the zoo is $17.3$ years old.   The population variance is $10.7$ years$^2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
You have found the following ages (in years) of all $6$ lizards at your local zoo:

 $\qquad1,\enspace 2,\enspace 2,\enspace 1,\enspace 3,\enspace 3$

 ** What is the average age of the lizards at your zoo? What is the variance?**  
*Round your answers to the nearest tenth.*

Average age:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 1]] years old 

  Variance:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 2]] years$^2$
hints: Because we have data for all $6$ lizards at the zoo, we are able to calculate the population mean $(\blue{\mu})$ and population variance $(\red{\sigma^2})$.

To find the population mean, add up the values of all $6$ ages and divide by $6$. 

  $ \blue{\mu} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{N}} x_i}{\green{N}} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{6}} x_i}{\green{6}} $
$ \blue{\mu} \quad = \quad \dfrac{1 + 2 + 2 + 1 + 3 + 3}{\green{6}} \quad = \quad \blue{2\text{ years old}} $

Find the squared deviations from the mean for each lizard. 

 Age
 
 $x_i$ | Distance from the mean $(x_i - \blue{\mu})$ | $(x_i - \blue{\mu})^2$ 
-|-|-
 $1$ year | $-1$ years | $1$ year$^2$ 
 $2$ years | $0$ years | $0$ years$^2$ 
 $2$ years | $0$ years | $0$ years$^2$ 
 $1$ year | $-1$ years | $1$ year$^2$ 
 $3$ years | $1$ year | $1$ year$^2$ 
 $3$ years | $1$ year | $1$ year$^2$

Because we used the population mean $(\blue{\mu})$ to compute the squared deviations from the mean, we can find the population variance $(\color{red}{\sigma^2})$, without introducing any bias, by simply averaging the squared deviations from the mean: 

  $ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{N}} (x_i - \blue{\mu})^2}{\green{N}} $
$ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{1} + \color{purple}{0} + \color{purple}{0} + \color{purple}{1} + \color{purple}{1} + \color{purple}{1}} {\green{6}} $
$ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{4}}{\green{6}} \quad = \quad \color{red}{0.67\text{ years}^2} $
The average lizard at the zoo is $2$ years old.   The population variance is $0.7$ years$^2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
You have found the following ages (in years) of all $5$ zebras at your local zoo:

 $\qquad8,\enspace 11,\enspace 17,\enspace 7,\enspace 19$

 **What is the average age of the zebras at your zoo? What is the variance?**  
*Round your answers to the nearest tenth.*

Average age:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 1]] years old 

  Variance:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 2]] years$^2$
hints: Because we have data for all $5$ zebras at the zoo, we are able to calculate the population mean $(\blue{\mu})$ and population variance $(\red{\sigma^2})$.

To find the population mean, add up the values of all $5$ ages and divide by $5$. 

  $ \blue{\mu} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{N}} x_i}{\green{N}} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{5}} x_i}{\green{5}} $
$ \blue{\mu} \quad = \quad \dfrac{8 + 11 + 17 + 7 + 19}{\green{5}} \quad = \quad \blue{12.4\text{ years old}} $

Find the squared deviations from the mean for each zebra. 

 Age
 
 $x_i$ | Distance from the mean $(x_i - \blue{\mu})$ | $(x_i - \blue{\mu})^2$ 
-|-|-
 $8$ years | $-4.4$ years | $19.36$ years$^2$ 
 $11$ years | $-1.4$ years | $1.96$ years$^2$ 
 $17$ years | $4.6$ years | $21.16$ years$^2$ 
 $7$ years | $-5.4$ years | $29.16$ years$^2$ 
 $19$ years | $6.6$ years | $43.56$ years$^2$

Because we used the population mean $(\blue{\mu})$ to compute the squared deviations from the mean, we can find the population variance $(\color{red}{\sigma^2})$, without introducing any bias, by simply averaging the squared deviations from the mean: 

  $ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{N}} (x_i - \blue{\mu})^2}{\green{N}} $
$ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{19.36} + \color{purple}{1.96} + \color{purple}{21.16} + \color{purple}{29.16} + \color{purple}{43.56}} {\green{5}} $
$ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{115.2}}{\green{5}} \quad = \quad \color{red}{23.04\text{ years}^2} $
The average zebra at the zoo is $12.4$ years old.   The population variance is $23$ years$^2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
You have found the following ages (in years) of all $5$ porcupines at your local zoo:

 $\qquad16,\enspace 10,\enspace 5,\enspace 7,\enspace 13$

 **What is the average age of the porcupines at your zoo? What is the variance?**  
*Round your answers to the nearest tenth.*

Average age:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 1]] years old 

  Variance:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 2]] years$^2$
hints: Because we have data for all $5$ porcupines at the zoo, we are able to calculate the population mean $(\blue{\mu})$ and population variance $(\red{\sigma^2})$.

To find the population mean, add up the values of all $5$ ages and divide by $5$. 

  $ \blue{\mu} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{N}} x_i}{\green{N}} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{5}} x_i}{\green{5}} $
$ \blue{\mu} \quad = \quad \dfrac{16 + 10 + 5 + 7 + 13}{\green{5}} \quad = \quad \blue{10.2\text{ years old}} $

Find the squared deviations from the mean for each porcupine. 

 Age
 
 $x_i$ | Distance from the mean $(x_i - \blue{\mu})$ | $(x_i - \blue{\mu})^2$ 
-|-|-
 $16$ years | $5.8$ years | $33.64$ years$^2$ 
 $10$ years | $-0.2$ years | $0.04$ years$^2$ 
 $5$ years | $-5.2$ years | $27.04$ years$^2$ 
 $7$ years | $-3.2$ years | $10.24$ years$^2$ 
 $13$ years | $2.8$ years | $7.84$ years$^2$

Because we used the population mean $(\blue{\mu})$ to compute the squared deviations from the mean, we can find the population variance $(\color{red}{\sigma^2})$, without introducing any bias, by simply averaging the squared deviations from the mean: 

  $ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{N}} (x_i - \blue{\mu})^2}{\green{N}} $
$ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{33.64} + \color{purple}{0.04} + \color{purple}{27.04} + \color{purple}{10.24} + \color{purple}{7.84}} {\green{5}} $
$ \color{red}{\sigma^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{78.8}}{\green{5}} \quad = \quad \color{red}{15.76\text{ years}^2} $
The average porcupine at the zoo is $10.2$ years old.   The population variance is $15.8$ years$^2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
You have found the following ages (in years) of $4$ zebras. The zebras are randomly selected from the $45$ zebras at your local zoo: 

 $\qquad14,\enspace 15,\enspace 9,\enspace 1$

 **Based on your sample, what is the average age of the zebras? What is the estimated variance of the ages?**  
*You may round your answers to the nearest tenth.*

Average age:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 1]] years old 

  Variance:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 2]] years$^2$
hints: Because we only have data for a small sample of the $45$ zebras, we are only able to estimate the population mean and variance by finding the sample mean $(\blue{\overline{x}})$ and sample variance $(\red{s^2})$.

To find the sample mean, add up the values of all $4$ samples and divide by $4$. 

  $ \blue{\overline{x}} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{n}} x_i}{\green{n}} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{4}} x_i}{\green{4}} $
$ \blue{\overline{x}} \quad = \quad \dfrac{14 + 15 + 9 + 1}{\green{4}} \quad = \quad \blue{9.75\text{ years old}} $

Find the squared deviations from the mean for each sample. Since we don't know the population mean, estimate the mean by using the sample mean we just calculated $(\blue{\overline{x}} = \blue{9.75\text{ years}})$.

Age
 
 $x_i$ | Distance from the mean $(x_i - \blue{\overline{x}})$ | $(x_i - \blue{\overline{x}})^2$ 
-|-|-
 $14$ years | $4.25$ years | $18.06$ years$^2$ 
 $15$ years | $5.25$ years | $27.56$ years$^2$ 
 $9$ years | $-0.75$ years | $0.56$ years$^2$ 
 $1$ year | $-8.75$ years | $76.56$ years$^2$

Normally we can find the variance $(\color{red}{s^2})$ by averaging the squared deviations from the mean. But remember we don't know the real population meanŌĆöwe had to estimate it by using the sample mean. 

  The age of any zebra in our sample is likely to be closer to the average of the $4$ zebras we looked at instead of the average of all the zebras in the zoo. Because of that, the squared deviations from the mean we calculated will probably underestimate the actual deviations from the population mean. 

  To compensate for this underestimation, rather than simply averaging the squared deviations from the mean, we total them and divide by $n - 1$. 

  $ \color{red}{s^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{n}} (x_i - \blue{\overline{x}})^2}{\green{n - 1}} $
$ \color{red}{s^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{18.06} + \color{purple}{27.56} + \color{purple}{0.56} + \color{purple}{76.56}} {\green{4 - 1}} $
$ \color{red}{s^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{122.74}}{\green{3}} \quad = \quad \color{red}{40.91\text{ years}^2} $
We can estimate that the average zebra at the zoo is $9.8$ years old.   The estimated variance of the zebras is $40.9$ years$^2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
You have found the following ages (in years) of $4$ zebras. The zebras are randomly selected from the $45$ zebras at your local zoo: 

 $\qquad7,\enspace 1,\enspace 9,\enspace 14$

 **Based on your sample, what is the average age of the zebras? What is the estimated variance of the ages?**  
*You may round your answers to the nearest tenth.*

Average age:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 1]] years old 

  Variance:
 
$\quad$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 2]] years$^2$
hints: Because we only have data for a small sample of the $45$ zebras, we are only able to estimate the population mean and variance by finding the sample mean $(\blue{\overline{x}})$ and sample variance $(\red{s^2})$.

To find the sample mean, add up the values of all $4$ samples and divide by $4$. 

  $ \blue{\overline{x}} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{n}} x_i}{\green{n}} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{4}} x_i}{\green{4}} $
$ \blue{\overline{x}} \quad = \quad \dfrac{7 + 1 + 9 + 14}{\green{4}} \quad = \quad \blue{7.75\text{ years old}} $

Find the squared deviations from the mean for each sample. Since we don't know the population mean, estimate the mean by using the sample mean we just calculated $(\blue{\overline{x}} = \blue{7.75\text{ years}})$.

Age
 
 $x_i$ | Distance from the mean $(x_i - \blue{\overline{x}})$ | $(x_i - \blue{\overline{x}})^2$ 
-|-|-
 $7$ years | $-0.75$ years | $0.56$ years$^2$ 
 $1$ year | $-6.75$ years | $45.56$ years$^2$ 
 $9$ years | $1.25$ years | $1.56$ years$^2$ 
 $14$ years | $6.25$ years | $39.06$ years$^2$

Normally we can find the variance $(\color{red}{s^2})$ by averaging the squared deviations from the mean. But remember we don't know the real population meanŌĆöwe had to estimate it by using the sample mean. 

  The age of any zebra in our sample is likely to be closer to the average of the $4$ zebras we looked at instead of the average of all the zebras in the zoo. Because of that, the squared deviations from the mean we calculated will probably underestimate the actual deviations from the population mean. 

  To compensate for this underestimation, rather than simply averaging the squared deviations from the mean, we total them and divide by $n - 1$. 

  $ \color{red}{s^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{n}} (x_i - \blue{\overline{x}})^2}{\green{n - 1}} $
$ \color{red}{s^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{0.56} + \color{purple}{45.56} + \color{purple}{1.56} + \color{purple}{39.06}} {\green{4 - 1}} $
$ \color{red}{s^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{86.74}}{\green{3}} \quad = \quad \color{red}{28.91\text{ years}^2} $
We can estimate that the average zebra at the zoo is $7.8$ years old.   The estimated variance of the zebras is $28.9$ years$^2$.

 $7$ years | $-3.2$ years | $10.24$ years$^2$ 
 $13$ years | $2.8$ years | $7.84$ years$^2$

Normally we can find the variance $(\color{red}{s^2})$ by averaging the squared deviations from the mean. But remember we don't know the real population meanŌĆöwe had to estimate it by using the sample mean. 

  The age of any porcupine in our sample is likely to be closer to the average of the $5$ porcupines we looked at instead of the average of all the porcupines in the zoo. Because of that, the squared deviations from the mean we calculated will probably underestimate the actual deviations from the population mean. 

  To compensate for this underestimation, rather than simply averaging the squared deviations from the mean, we total them and divide by $n - 1$. 

  $ \color{red}{s^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\sum\limits_{i=1}^{\green{n}} (x_i - \blue{\overline{x}})^2}{\green{n - 1}} $
$ \color{red}{s^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{33.64} + \color{purple}{0.04} + \color{purple}{27.04} + \color{purple}{10.24} + \color{purple}{7.84}} {\green{5 - 1}} $
$ \color{red}{s^2} \quad = \quad \dfrac{\color{purple}{78.8}}{\green{4}} \quad = \quad \color{red}{19.7\text{ years}^2} $
We can estimate that the average porcupine at the zoo is $10.2$ years old.   The estimated variance of the porcupines is $19.7$ years$^2$.
